title: Terpsichore fabispora
intro:
There is limited information on the plant Terpsichore fabispora. However, Terpsichore is a genus of ferns that are often referred to as "antillean tree ferns." One of the species in the Terpsichore genus, Terpsichore maynardi, is known to be used in traditional medicine.

One of the ferns in the Terpsichore genus, Terpsichore maynardi, has been reported to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may contribute to the fern's traditional use in treating various health conditions, including wounds and infections.

The bioactive constituents of Terpsichore maynardi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

The most common herbal preparations made from Terpsichore maynardi include teas and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including wounds and infections.
